name: @radzor/magic-link-auth
description: Passwordless authentication via email magic links. Generates signed tokens, sends links via email, and verifies them on callback. Zero-dependency token management with configurable TTL and single-use enforcement.

inputs.name: secret
inputs.description: Secret key used to sign and verify magic link tokens (HMAC-SHA256).

inputs.name: baseUrl
inputs.description: Base URL for magic link callback (e.g. https://app.example.com/auth/verify).

inputs.name: singleUse
inputs.description: Invalidate token after first successful verification.

outputs.name: verifiedEmail
outputs.description: Email address of the successfully authenticated user.

outputs.name: tokenMeta
outputs.type: { email: string; expiresAt: number; used: boolean }
outputs.description: Token metadata including email, expiry, and usage status.

events.name: onLinkSent
events.description: Fired when a magic link is generated and ready to send.

events.name: onVerified
events.description: Fired when a magic link token is successfully verified.

events.name: onExpired
events.description: Fired when a token is used after its TTL has elapsed.

events.name: onError
events.description: Fired on token generation or verification error.

actions.name: generateLink
actions.returns: Promise<{ url: string; token: string; expiresAt: number }>
actions.description: Generate a signed magic link URL for the given email. Send this URL to the user via email.

actions.name: verifyToken

params.name: token
params.description: Token extracted from the magic link callback URL.

actions.description: Verify a magic link token. Returns the email if valid, throws if expired or already used.

actions.name: revokeToken
actions.description: Manually invalidate a token before it expires.

llm.constraints: generateLink() only creates the URL — you must send it via @radzor/email-send or similar. Tokens are stored in-memory by default; restart clears all pending tokens. For multi-instance deployments, use an external store.
